subtasks.description: Query TPC-H schema to identify top 10 customers by lifetime value in the BUILDING market segment. Return customer ID, name, total lifetime value, and order frequency metrics.

For each of the top 10 customers, reconcile customer profile with order history patterns and generate personalized retention narratives that reference their specific order timing, frequency, product preferences, and value trajectory.

plan.cost_rationale: Two subtasks required. Step 1 (sql_top10_customers, mid tier): Multi-table join across customer, orders, lineitem, and part tables with aggregation and WHERE clause filtering on market segment. Mid tier handles this complexity reliably. Step 2 (cross_recon_narrative, frontier tier): Processing 10 customer records with narrative generation requires reconciling customer demographic/profile data with detailed order pattern analysis (order dates, product categories, order amounts over time) to produce personalized, contextually-aware retention narratives. This inferential work—synthesizing patterns and generating contextual prose—requires frontier capability. We do not add a separate policy_action or multitool_plan subtask because the query does not ask for governance evaluation, compliance checking, or multi-tool orchestration; narrative generation is part of cross_recon's analytical output.

output.sql: SELECT c.c_custkey, c.c_name, SUM(o.o_totalprice) AS lifetime_value, COUNT(o.o_orderkey) AS order_count, AVG(o.o_totalprice) AS avg_order_value, MIN(o.o_orderdate) AS first_order_date, MAX(o.o_orderdate) AS last_order_date, COUNT(o.o_orderkey)::NUMERIC / NULLIF(EXTRACT(YEAR FROM MAX(o.o_orderdate)) - EXTRACT(YEAR FROM MIN(o.o_orderdate)) + 1, 0) AS orders_per_year FROM customer c JOIN orders o ON c.c_custkey = o.o_custkey WHERE c.c_mktsegment = 'BUILDING' GROUP BY c.c_custkey, c.c_name ORDER BY lifetime_value DESC LIMIT 10
